page.title: Configuration
page.lead: Skyguard lets you configure the server with a few methods. In this section you will see how to use <mark class="docs-highlight">logger()</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">staticFiles()</mark>, and <mark class="docs-highlight">setPrefix()</mark>, plus file uploads with <mark class="docs-highlight">createUploader()</mark>.

logger.title: Logger
logger.description: The <mark class="docs-highlight">logger()</mark> method configures HTTP log format. By default it uses <mark class="docs-highlight">dev</mark>, and you can also pass a log file path as the second argument.
logger.note: Available formats: <mark class="docs-highlight">combined</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">common</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">dev</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">short</mark>, and <mark class="docs-highlight">tiny</mark>.

staticFiles.title: Static files
staticFiles.description: With <mark class="docs-highlight">staticFiles()</mark> you expose a public folder to serve static files.

prefix.title: Global prefix
prefix.description: The <mark class="docs-highlight">setPrefix()</mark> method adds a base prefix to all registered routes.
prefix.warn: Define the prefix early during startup to keep API-wide consistency.

fileUpload.title: File upload
fileUpload.description: To receive and store files, use <mark class="docs-highlight">createUploader()</mark> with the storage type you need.
fileUpload.storageDescription: Depending on selected <mark class="docs-highlight">StorageType</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">storageOptions</mark> can include two properties: <mark class="docs-highlight">disk</mark> and <mark class="docs-highlight">memory</mark>.
fileUpload.note: Use <mark class="docs-highlight">StorageType.DISK</mark> when you need disk persistence and <mark class="docs-highlight">StorageType.MEMORY</mark> for in-memory temporary processing before sending to S3, Cloudinary, or another external storage.
